---
title: "3.5 Methods: How to Write Them"
subtitle: "Part 3: Setters and Parameters"
format:
  clean-revealjs:
    slide-number: true
    css: unit3-shared.css
lang: zh-CN
---

## Course Directory

### Return to the course outline

[← Back to AP CSA / 返回课程目录](../../index.html)

## Topic Intro

### Methods can change state or calculate from inputs

This deck focuses on:

::: {.tight-list}
- <span class="term">mutators</span> or setters
- parameters and arguments
- methods that return calculated values
:::

These methods connect class behavior to Unit 1 method signatures and Unit 2 algorithms.

## Mutators / Setters

### A setter changes object state

![Getter and setter comparison](../../assets/3-class-creation/3-5-methods-how-to-write-them/get-set-comparison.png){fig-align="center" width="58%"}

```java
public void setGradeLevel(int newGradeLevel)
{
    gradeLevel = newGradeLevel;
}
```

A setter is usually `void` and takes a parameter with the new value.

## Setter Design

### Do not add setters automatically

Only write a setter when outside code should be allowed to change that value.

```java
public void setGpa(double newGpa)
{
    if (newGpa >= 0.0 && newGpa <= 4.0)
    {
        gpa = newGpa;
    }
}
```

The method controls how state can change.

## Quick Check

### Pick the correct setter signature

Which method header best sets an `int gradeLevel`?

::: {.tight-list}
- A. `public int setGradeLevel()`
- B. `public void setGradeLevel(int newGradeLevel)`
- C. `public String setGradeLevel(String newGradeLevel)`
- D. `private void getGradeLevel(int newGradeLevel)`
:::

Answer: <span class="mark">B</span>.

## Parameters and Arguments

### Arguments are copied into parameters

![Arguments become parameters](../../assets/3-class-creation/3-5-methods-how-to-write-them/method-param-arg.png){fig-align="center" width="62%"}

::: {.tight-list}
- a <span class="term">parameter</span> is declared in the method header
- an <span class="term">argument</span> is supplied in the method call
- number, order, and type must match the signature
:::

## Parameter Trace

### Follow the value into the method

```java
public void setName(String newName)
{
    name = newName;
}

student.setName("Nora");
```

Trace:

::: {.tight-list}
- argument value: `"Nora"`
- parameter variable: `newName`
- assignment: `name = newName`
- object state after call: `name` stores `"Nora"`
:::

## Methods with Calculated Return Values

### A method can use parameters and return a result

```java
public boolean contains(String phrase, String target)
{
    return phrase.indexOf(target) >= 0;
}
```

This is not a getter because it does not just return an instance variable. It computes a result from input values.

## Code Task

### Find a substring

```java
public class StringFind
{
    public static boolean containsIgnoreCase(String phrase, String target)
    {
        String lowerPhrase = phrase.toLowerCase();
        String lowerTarget = target.toLowerCase();
        return lowerPhrase.indexOf(lowerTarget) >= 0;
    }
}
```

Test with `containsIgnoreCase("Computer Science", "science")`.

## Classroom Check

### A complete answer should...

::: {.tight-list}
- explain how a setter changes object state
- choose a correct setter header for a variable type
- distinguish parameters from arguments
- trace an argument value into a parameter variable
- explain how a method can return a calculated value rather than a stored field
:::

## End

### Continue or return to the course outline

[Next: Class Pet Challenge](3-5-part-4-class-pet-challenge.html)  
[← Back to AP CSA / 返回课程目录](../../index.html)
